description: A study and application of concepts and disciplines that foster spiritual growth and transformation essential for being a disciple of Jesus Christ. The course emphasizes experiential learning focusing on spiritual disciplines such as prayer, fasting, solitude, meditation, study, and forgiveness. Themes include nurturing desire to know God intimately, believers’ identity in Christ, God's grace, and biblical/theological study of John 15 and Romans 8.
teacher_name: Rachel Harlan, M.A.; Caleb Harlan, M.A.; Kate Trezise

description: Introduction to course overview and discipleship emphasis on desire to know God.

assignment_title: READ: Foster Ch 1 (pp.1-11); “Fan or Follower” (excerpt in ST Binder)
description: Reading on spiritual disciplines and the concept of followship as a disciple.
materials: Foster, Richard. Celebration of Discipline, Chapter 1 (pp. 1-11)
ST Binder excerpt: Fan or Follower

assignment_title: READ: Moser “Just Like Jesus” (pp. 7-24) – AT TAP
description: Study focus on prayer as a biblical strategy for spiritual growth.
materials: Moser, Phil. Just Like Jesus, pp. 7-24

description: Focus on the Word as a spiritual discipline and biblical strategy.
materials: Moser, Phil. Just Like Jesus, pp. 7-9, 25-39

description: Oral recitation of assigned scripture passages for memory checkpoint 1.

assignment_title: WRITE: Advanced organizer “Sabbath” (Do 1st, tan sheet in ST binder), READ: Barton Ch 8 “Sabbath” (Do 2nd)
description: Study and reflection on the Sabbath with written assignment and reading.
materials: Barton, Ruth Haley. Sacred Rhythms, Chapter 8: Sabbath

assignment_title: READ: Foster ch 7; Buchanan ch 17 (for credit students)
description: Exploration of solitude and silence as spiritual disciplines.
materials: Foster, Richard. Celebration of Discipline, Chapter 7
Buchanan, Mark. Your God is Too Safe, Chapter 17 (for credit students)

assignment_title: READ: Foster ch 4; Piper’s A Hunger for God: “Preface,” “Introduction,” and “Conclusion” (excerpt in ST Binder) – AT TIOGA when due
description: Study of fasting as a spiritual discipline with related readings.
materials: Foster, Richard. Celebration of Discipline, Chapter 4
Piper, John. A Hunger for God (Preface, Introduction, Conclusion) - ST Binder excerpt

assignment_title: READ: Voskamp ch 3 (excerpt in ST Binder)
description: Focus on the practice of Eucharisteo (gratitude) and study of John 15.
materials: Voskamp, Ann. One Thousand Gifts, Chapter 3 (ST Binder excerpt)

description: Oral recitation for second scripture memory checkpoint.

assignment_title: READ: Yancey ch 2 & ch 4 (for credit students)
description: Study of God's grace in Christian life and spiritual disciplines.
materials: Yancey, Philip. What's So Amazing About Grace, Chapters 2 & 4

assignment_title: READ: Anderson Victory Over the Darkness ch 2, “The Whole Gospel” (excerpt in ST Binder)
description: Exploration of the believer’s identity in Christ and applying these truths for spiritual growth.
materials: Anderson, Neil T. Victory Over the Darkness, Chapter 2
ST Binder excerpt: The Whole Gospel

assignment_title: READ: Peace Pursuit (excerpt in ST Binder) – In NH when due
description: Biblical approach to conflict, reconciliation, and spiritual peace.
Study of the Holy Spirit's role in spiritual transformation.
materials: Moser, Phil. Just Like Jesus, pp. 41-54

description: Written paper due discussing the topic of Identity in Christ.

assignment_title: READ: Foster Ch 1; Calhoun “Retreat” (pp. 77-79), “Meditation” (pp. 191-193), "Prayer of Recollection" (pp. 281-283). Bring personal journals to class.
description: Retreat and meditation practices with spiritual journaling.
materials: Foster, Richard. Celebration of Discipline, Chapter 1
Calhoun selections: Retreat (pp. 77-79), Meditation (pp. 191-193), Prayer of Recollection (pp. 281-283)

assignment_title: WRITE: Semester Break Plan due @ Exit Talks; WRITE: Semester Break Reflection
description: Prepare and submit spiritual formation plan and reflection for semester break. Bring journals to Exit Talks.
